response.justification: Exceptional candidate with 15+ years of experience, significantly exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Demonstrates mastery of core stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, testing, Git) and explicitly has current hands-on experience with AI/agent infrastructure and MCP development—a rare required skill. Strong track record across full stack, multiple industries, and proven commitment to engineering excellence (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). Minor concerns: Nest.js and Vitest not explicitly mentioned despite being primary stack frameworks, and career trajectory from Staff to Senior warrants discussion about role fit and expectations.
strengths: AI/agent infrastructure and MCP experience directly addresses required skill, with proven hands-on implementation in current role at re:cinq
15+ years shipping production features across full stack (backend, frontend, databases, cloud, DevOps) with consistent emphasis on testing, code quality, and engineering best practices

Demonstrated mastery of TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, Docker, AWS, and Git; plus extensive multi-language fluency (Java, DLang, Python-adjacent experience) enabling rapid adoption of secondary stack components
concerns: Nest.js and Vitest not explicitly mentioned in resume; while clearly capable of learning these specific frameworks, this creates a slight gap against primary stack requirements and should be confirmed in technical discussion

Currently Staff Software Engineer with multiple promotions and expanding scope (contributing to AI infrastructure, developer tooling, and org-wide decisions)—lateral or downward move to Senior role raises questions about motivation, compensation expectations, and long-term fit that should be explored

Security and authentication/authorization expertise not evident in resume despite being explicit required skill; foundational knowledge should be verified through technical conversation, particularly around modern patterns and compliance
